name: iRobot Roomba
short_description: An autonomous disc-shaped robot vacuum that drives itself around the home to clean floors.
long_description: First released in 2002, the Roomba is the consumer robot that put autonomous machines into ordinary households. Early models cleaned via reactive bump-and-turn behaviors, while later generations (e.g. the 980 and i-series) added forward-looking cameras with vSLAM-based mapping and navigation, app control, and self-emptying docks. As one of the first mass-market mobile robots, it is a foundational example of physical AI in daily life: a low-legibility appliance that perceives, decides, and acts in a shared human space with little supervision.

images.caption: Top view of an iRobot Roomba (model 782) robot vacuum.
images.alt_text: A round, dark grey iRobot Roomba robotic vacuum cleaner photographed from directly above, showing its circular body and central control buttons.

sources.title: Roomba
sources.access_notes: Overview of Roomba history, 2002 release, and vSLAM navigation in later models.

sources.title: iRobot Brings Visual Mapping and Navigation to the Roomba 980
sources.access_notes: Details the camera-based vSLAM navigation and mapping introduced on the Roomba 980.

interactions.title: It photographs the hazard and asks what to do
interactions.description: When a camera-equipped Roomba meets an unfamiliar obstacle on the floor, it snaps a picture, sends it to the app after the run, and lets you tag the object as a one-time mess to retry later or a permanent keep-out.

interactions.title: Trained to swerve around the dog's accident
interactions.description: The j7+ runs an on-board vision model (trained partly on play-dough mock-ups) that recognizes solid pet waste and drives around it instead of through it; iRobot backs it with a free-replacement guarantee if it fails.

interactions.title: Draw a no-go box on the house map
interactions.description: Once the robot has learned an Imprint Smart Map, you drag rectangular Keep-Out Zones over the pet bowls, the cable nest, or the kids' Lego corner so it learns the unspoken 'house rules' and never enters them.

interactions.title: Drive it over to peek at an empty room
interactions.description: With the j7's beta Remote Check-In, you steer the vacuum to any spot on its map from the app and open a live video stream through its obstacle camera — an improvised roving check on the house or a pet while you're out.

interactions.title: The cat commandeers it as a parade float
interactions.description: Owners discovered cats will perch on a running Roomba and ride it around the floor; one cat in a shark costume, trailed by a duckling, became a multi-million-view internet fixture and spawned a whole genre of animals-on-Roombas videos.
interactions.source_label: CBS News — shark-cat on a Roomba

interactions.title: The 'poopocalypse' — autonomy as a liability
interactions.description: Before poop detection existed, a Roomba that hit a pet's overnight accident would faithfully smear it across every room; one owner described 25-foot poop trails on 'every conceivable surface,' a viral cautionary tale about turning a machine loose unsupervised.

interactions.title: The cleaner that quietly maps your home
interactions.description: The same vSLAM map that lets the robot clean efficiently is also a floor plan of your house — furniture, room sizes, layout — and a 2017 plan to potentially share those maps with big tech firms turned the vacuum into a privacy flashpoint.

interactions.title: Gutted and rebuilt as a research robot
interactions.description: Hobbyists and labs strip the vacuum down to its drive base — or buy iRobot's own Create 3, a Roomba sans dustbin — and program it with Python and ROS 2 to do SLAM, mapping, and autonomy experiments well beyond cleaning.

interactions.title: Standing patrol on a recognized hazard
interactions.description: Because the robot already classifies floor objects and can stream from where it parks, it could be told to roll to a recurring trouble spot — a leak-prone water heater, a pet's feeding mat — and send a snapshot on a schedule, reusing its existing vision and Remote Check-In capabilities.
